meta_description: Instant quotes for electroless nickel plated parts. Parts are machined to Ra1.6Âµm. Resistant to wear, abrasion and corrosion (non-cosmetic).

sections.heading: Electroless nickel plating services
sections.text: Surface finishes Electroless nickel plating services Electroless nickel plating is a process that deposits an alloy of nickel-phosphorus onto the surface of a metal. This process is chemical only, so does not involve the use of electricity. Get instant quote

sections.text: Example of an electroless nickel plated part

sections.heading: Design considerations for electroless nickel plating
sections.text: Design considerations for electroless nickel plating Masking: Electroless nickel plating does not have a significant dimensional impact, therefore masking parts is not necessary.

sections.heading: The Protolabs Network electroless nickel plating process
sections.text: The Protolabs Network electroless nickel plating process Electroless nickel plating is a wear, abrasion and corrosion resistant finish that is compatible with aluminum, stainless steel and mild steel. Hereâs how to apply electroless nickel plating: Parts are first washed and degreased to remove any contaminants, cleaning away any grease or oil After cleaning is completed, the substrate is activated with another chemical, an acid, to remove oxides and activate the surface. Parts are then dipped in the plating chemical solution, depositing the nickel-phosphorus alloy on the parts until desired thickness is reached
